mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects healthy corporate output and forward earnings capacity, which equity markets price in incrementally over short horizons, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when INDPRO momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3006 as of t-2mo, consistent with late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 1-month change and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO momentum periods show no statistically significant mean return difference versus negative momentum periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0% during an active hiking cycle, rising discount rates begin to materially compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days relative to periods when the funds rate is below this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of t-1mo, approaching threshold amid Fed hiking cycle
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns after FEDFUNDS crosses 2.0% are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) than returns in the sub-2.0% regime across at least two independent tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is near cyclical lows (below 4.2%) and the federal funds rate is simultaneously rising, the joint signal indicates the Fed is actively fighting wage-driven inflation risk, raising the probability of policy overtightening and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither UNRATE nor FEDFUNDS alone at moderate levels carries this implication.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of t-1mo, near cycle lows while FEDFUNDS is on a rising trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint low-UNRATE/rising-FEDFUNDS episodes produce forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from or higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) over at least two market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand ahead of broader economic slowdown, and this leading-indicator deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. In early or mid-cycle regimes, HOUST declines instead reflect noise or supply constraints and carry no reliable signal.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1350K as of t-2mo amid rising mortgage rates in a late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: HOUST 3-month declines exceeding 5% during late-cycle regimes fail to precede negative 60-day SPX forward returns in a majority of instances (win rate <= 50%) across historical late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a 150,000 monthly-gain trigger signals an inflection toward labor market slack, foreshadowing weaker consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is breached.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS monthly gain falls below 150000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148912K as of t-1mo, near the proposed deceleration threshold
failure_condition: Instances of PAYEMS 3-month average gains falling below 150,000 are not followed by negative mean 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least three distinct episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days whenever RSAFS momentum is positive, independent of any threshold condition.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971 million as of t-2mo showing continued consumer spending growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 1-month growth and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth is positive but consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) simultaneously accelerates above a 2.5% year-over-year pace, the combination signals emerging cost-push stagflation risk that compresses profit margins despite headline output growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone at these levels signals margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.857 as of t-1mo reflecting inflation running above the Fed's 2% target
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising input costs
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of positive INDPRO growth and CPIAUCSL YoY above 2.5% do not produce negative mean 20-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising trend in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation combined with an already elevated FEDFUNDS level increases the perceived probability of continued aggressive rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. In easing or neutral regimes, the same inflation uptick does not carry this negative implication because policy response is muted.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY trending upward for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.857 and FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of t-1mo, consistent with an active tightening cycle amid rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during rising-CPI/tightening-cycle episodes are not significantly more negative (p > 0.05) than returns during rising-CPI episodes occurring in easing or neutral monetary regimes.
